question_text: Neptune orbits the Sun once every 165 Earth years, while Earth orbits once every 1 year. Explain why Neptune takes so much longer to orbit the Sun than Earth does, using the terms 'distance' and 'orbital period'.
correct_answer: Neptune is much further from the Sun than Earth. The further a planet is from the Sun, the longer its orbital period (the time it takes to complete one orbit). Because Neptune is so far away, it has to travel a much greater distance and moves more slowly in its orbit, so it takes 165 years to complete one orbit.
explanation: This demonstrates Kepler's Third Law: planets that are further from the Sun take longer to orbit. Neptune is about 30 times further from the Sun than Earth, so it has both a longer path to travel AND moves more slowly, resulting in a much longer orbital period.
